data.settleTest: NVIDIA's own DGX SuperPOD B300 Reference Architecture (dated 2025-10-30) shows the flagship GPU deployment shipping in standard EIA racks with three 2U rack PDUs each — a purchasable/published artifact that directly settles whether GPU racks skip PDUs today. Combined with the Grand View PDU market report attributing growth to AI/GPU workloads, the load-bearing premise ('GPU growth arrives PDU-less') is falsified now, not over time.

proposedCorrection.rationale: The claim's mechanism (GPU racks skip PDUs) is contradicted by NVIDIA's B300 reference architecture using rack PDUs, so the full 0.29 remains defensible as PDU-addressable; only a modest downside band reflects residual busbar/DB-mix uncertainty rather than the claimed structural haircut.

researchQueries.query: NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD B300 and GB200/GB300 reference architectures: share of deployments using rack PDU AC power vs DC busbar power shelves
Grand View / Omdia rack PDU market reports quantifying AI/GPU-driven enterprise on-premise rack PDU attach rates 2024-2026
